title: Self-Improving Agents for Physical AI
description: [KNOWN] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] Agentic environment-generation harness for Physical AI: route text requests, image/video anchors, internet asset and dataset retrieval, and cross-simulator migration through reusable Skills and MCP tools, preferring verified asset reuse and parameter filling before rigid or articulated 3D generation.
summary: [KNOWN] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] The four V1 workflows retain bounded acceptance evidence: Text2Env Stage 0-5, image/video Anchor2Env, verified asset discovery/import, and Open-X-Sim L0-L3 transfer. [KNOWN] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] The current phase integrates them into one agentic harness with reusable Skill/MCP contracts, a selection-first asset route, a learned rigid/articulated 3D-generation fallback, and regression-gated cross-simulator compilation. [KNOWN] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] Higher-fidelity open-world generation and policy/controller equivalence remain open.
asset_alt: Agentic Physical AI harness loop with reusable environment-generation Skills and MCP tools for text, visual anchors, asset reuse or generation, validation, and cross-simulator transfer.
asset_caption: [FRAME] Phase 2 harness: orchestrate reusable Skills/MCP around four routes, reuse and parameterize verified assets first, fall back to rigid or articulated 3D generation when needed, then compile, validate, replay and transfer with regression gates.
details: [KNOWN] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] Four user-facing routes define the current environment-generation scope: Text2Env, image/video Anchor2Env, automatic internet asset or dataset retrieval, and cross-simulator environment migration.
[FRAME] The orchestrator follows a selection-first policy: discover, match, select and fill parameters for verified existing assets before invoking a generative fallback.

[FRAME] The asset-reuse branch records candidate ranking, rejected candidates, license and provenance, hashes, units, axes, scale, visual and collision geometry, material, mass and inertia, stable poses, and joint metadata or typed unknowns.

[FRAME] The generation branch handles rigid and articulated objects separately and must emit simulator-ready visual and collision geometry, scale, physics metadata, joint topology and limits, provenance, hashes, and validation evidence.

[FRAME] The reusable Skill/MCP layer exposes discover, match, fill, generate, compile, validate and transfer through typed contracts with versioned dependencies, bounded retries, run_state, events, artifact manifests, failure codes and rollback.

[KNOWN] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] Cross-simulator reuse only claims a declared common subset plus measured migration losses; contact, rendering, controller behavior and task outcomes are not equivalent unless their explicit gates pass.

references.title: Drift: robotics-native simulation engineering agent
references.notes: Direct product baseline for the Text2Env execution loop. Drift takes natural-language robotics requests, generates or edits URDF/SDF/MJCF and ROS2 workspace files, launches Gazebo or MuJoCo, observes simulator and ROS state, validates physics/runtime behavior, and attempts build or simulation repair. Its prompt -> workspace -> run -> inspect -> repair workflow strongly overlaps our Text2Env compiler and executable harness, so benchmark against it rather than claiming prompt-to-simulation itself as novelty. Differentiate through image/video Anchor2Env, autonomous web asset discovery with provenance and conversion, typed SceneSpec/AssetSpec contracts, measured Open-X-Sim migration loss, environment-pool governance, and regression-gated self-improvement.

references.title: Drift documentation: Introduction and current capability boundary
references.notes: Implementation reference for local CLI orchestration, DRIFT.md/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md project context, reusable team skills, multi-step workspace edits, live ROS2 inspection, command visibility, and build-error recovery. Current v1.0.15 documentation describes Ubuntu x86_64, ROS2, Gazebo Harmonic and MuJoCo as the supported path; the docs index says not to rely on Isaac Sim today even though the marketing page advertises it as coming soon. No official evidence was found for image/video-conditioned scene generation, autonomous internet asset search, semantics-preserving cross-simulator migration, policy training in the current beta, or regression-tested harness promotion, so keep those as explicit project differentiators and evaluation gates.

references.title: General Agent: A Self-Evolving, Synthetic Agent Environment
references.notes: Reference for synthesizer-solver task generation, DB/tools/instruction/verification task anatomy, gold-solution replay validation, solver pass-rate difficulty bands, and large synthetic tool-calling corpora.

references.title: Learning to Build the Environment: Self-Evolving Reasoning RL via Verifiable Environment Synthesis
references.notes: Reference for reusable executable environments, stable solve-verify asymmetry, staged validation, semantic review, solver-relative difficulty calibration, novelty gating, and active environment-pool rotation.

references.title: FATE: Closed-Loop Feasibility-Aware Task Generation with Active Repair for Physically Grounded Robotic Curricula
references.notes: Reference for closed-loop validation-and-refinement, static scene affordance/layout auditing, simulated embodied feasibility checks, and active repair of infeasible tasks.

references.title: RoboGen: Towards Unleashing Infinite Data for Automated Robot Learning via Generative Simulation
references.notes: Reference for a propose-generate-supervise-learn cycle: task proposal, scene generation, training supervision generation, and skill learning through generative simulation.

references.title: RoboTwin 2.0: Expert Code Generation Pipeline
references.notes: Reference for code-agent task generation, repeated simulated execution, execution-log/VLM observation, error localization, and simulation-in-the-loop code repair.

references.title: Genesis World 1.0
references.notes: Candidate simulator base to evaluate later. Reference for unified multi-physics, photo-realistic Nyx rendering, Quadrants compiler, fast rollout, rigid/deformable/contact-rich manipulation, and possible lower sim-to-real gap.

references.title: Quadrants
references.notes: Reference compiler layer for large-scale physics simulation and robotics workloads across CUDA, Vulkan, Apple Metal, ROCm, x86, and ARM64.

references.title: Nyx renderer for Genesis
references.notes: Reference rendering layer for GPU-accelerated path tracing as a Genesis camera sensor, including PBR materials, HDRI/analytic lighting, 3D Gaussian splat assets, multi-camera and multi-environment rendering.

references.title: Articraft: An Agentic System for Scalable Articulated 3D Asset Generation
references.notes: Reference for agentic programmatic generation of articulated 3D assets. The system has an LLM write asset programs against an SDK for parts, geometry, joints, and tests, then validates outputs in a restricted harness; useful as a candidate AgenticSim asset-expansion route.

references.title: Articraft-10K asset dataset
references.notes: Candidate asset-library source for SceneAgent selection2env. Current workspace parses it into `artifacts/adapter_catalog/articraft10k_manifest.json` with 9996 searchable URDF archive entries and now has five selected archive probes under `runs/articraft_archive_probe_*/probe_report.json`: weight bench, microscope, and monitor pass SAPIEN `PhysxArticulation` load plus 20 physics steps; front-load washing machine and Zippo lighter load/step but fail the missing-collision-geometry gate. Do not treat Articraft as solved generation2env until broader scale, material, and task-suitability validation pass across more samples.

references.title: RoboTwin 2.0 official repository
references.notes: Active near-term demo base for Self-Improving Agents: build the tabletop Text2Env demo, API list, manual-program new-task path, data collection dry run, and Pi / OpenVLA-OFT training/evaluation hooks before treating it as a broader AgenticSim backend.

references.title: Dual-Sim: RoboTwin tasks migrated to IsaacSim
references.notes: User-provided implementation reference for bringing RoboTwin-style tasks into IsaacSim. Public fetch returned 404 during this dashboard update, so the first TODO step is to confirm access/clone; if available, use its task conversion, asset, reset/step, and verifier structure as a concrete merge path into AgenticSim.

references.title: SceneSmith: Agentic Generation of Simulation-Ready Indoor Scenes
references.notes: Reference pattern for the RoboTwin2 Text2Env demo, not the full implementation target. SceneSmith generates full indoor simulation-ready scenes with designer/critic/orchestrator VLM agents; our v0 borrows that staged loop but weakens it to tabletop manipulation: text -> table env description -> RoboTwin2 task program -> simulator smoke / data collection / policy evaluation.

references.title: Self-Improving Agents proposal slides: Orchestrator + shared Blackboard loop
references.notes: User-provided bound deck for the Self-Improving project. Current intro should follow the slide framing: a central Orchestrator plus shared Blackboard/Memory coordinates Env Setup, Data Collect, Policy Training, Eval Agent, and Diagnosis/Analysis; the loop is Setup -> Collect -> Train -> Evaluate -> Diagnose -> targeted Setup, with structured specs plus natural-language rationales as auditable inter-agent messages.

references.title: Dashboard asset: robotics simulator taxonomy
references.notes: User-provided figure credited to Tongzhou Mu. Use as a backend-selection taxonomy for AgenticSim / Text2Env: distinguish task APIs, asset collections, simulator frameworks, renderers, and physics engines before choosing RoboTwin2, Isaac Sim, Genesis, ManiSkill/SAPIEN, MuJoCo, Habitat, RLBench, or CoppeliaSim.

references.title: Video2Sim2Real: Full-Stack Autonomous Dexterous Skill Acquisition from a Single Human Video
references.notes: Real2Sim2Real reference for turning one human manipulation video into an executable robot skill. Useful pieces for AgenticSim: foundation-model digital-twin reconstruction, robot/object motion priors, object-centric keyframe anchor optimization, IL-based recalibration from noisy real point clouds, residual RL for finger-level contact adaptation, and CuRobo-style collision-aware spatial generalization.

references.title: Agentic Real2Sim: Physics-based World Modeling with Vision-Language Agents
references.notes: Direct episode-level Real2Sim reference for PEARL `/gen-env -> /collect`: it converts a synchronized interaction recording into a replayable episode twin that binds observations, actors/end effectors, geometry and appearance, time-indexed simulator states, physical/alignment parameters, a simulator backend, and acceptance traces. Map it into the existing Anchor2Env `interaction_episode` adapter and EnvironmentPackage/CollectionBundle lineage rather than creating a new command; reuse its pattern of bounded VLM orchestration, deterministic perception/simulation tools, critics, and limited retries. Current boundary: the site still marks code as coming soon; rigid DROID-100 reports 48 success, 8 partial, and 44 failure for the open 31B backend under a VLM-judge success-finder heuristic, deformable/humanoid evidence is qualitative, and downstream policy-learning/evaluation benefit remains future work.

references.title: video2sim-forge: RGB-D/video-to-sim asset forge
references.notes: Implementation reference for the /gen-env missing-asset branch. The repo is scoped to video/RGB-D capture -> object prompts, SAM3 masks, SAM3D meshes, world-frame poses, scene JSON, URDF export, and approximate physics metadata; it explicitly does not cover downstream policy training or closed-loop sim2real. Use it as a forge backend under PEARL's /gen-env, not as a replacement for /collect, /train, or /evaluate.

references.title: NeuMaTeX: Extracting Neural Materials from Multi-view Images
references.notes: Reference for the /gen-env material and texture branch. NeuMaTeX extracts spatially varying neural materials from multi-view images with differentiable inverse rendering and relightable material decomposition; use it as a visual-material sidecar for forged assets, not as a substitute for geometry, collision, joint, scale, or physics validation.

references.title: InternData-A1: Pioneering High-Fidelity Synthetic Data for Pre-training Generalist Policy
references.notes: User-flagged CVPR reference. Synthetic-data scaling reference for VLA pretraining: 630k trajectories / 7,433 hours across 4 embodiments, 18 skills, 70 tasks, and 227 scenes; covers rigid, articulated, deformable, and fluid manipulation and reports simulation-only pretraining competitive with strong real-robot pi-data baselines plus zero-shot sim-to-real transfer.

references.title: InternDataEngine: High-Fidelity Synthetic Data Generator for Robotic Manipulation
references.notes: Implementation reference for scalable embodied-data production on Isaac Sim. Unifies InternData-A1 physical interaction, InternData-M1 semantic task/scene generation, and Nimbus asynchronous scheduling; exports rich annotations such as bounding boxes, segmentation masks, and keypoints, with layout/texture/structure/lighting randomization and 2-3x throughput scheduling claims.

references.title: MolmoAct 2: An open foundation for robots that work in the real world
references.notes: Candidate open reasoning VLA base. MolmoAct 2 frames itself as an Action Reasoning Model that reasons in 3D before acting, releases models/data/code/training recipes, integrates with LeRobot, and includes the 720-hour bimanual YAM dataset. Important for a self-improving direction because it gives an inspectable reasoning/action backbone rather than a closed VLA.

references.title: vla-eval: A Unified Evaluation Harness for Vision-Language-Action Models
references.notes: Primary open implementation reference for PEARL `/evaluate`, reviewed at upstream commit `37cac713` after the v0.4.0 release. It decouples containerized robot-simulation benchmarks from standalone model servers through a WebSocket/msgpack protocol, Benchmark and EpisodeRunner adapters, sync/live execution, action chunking, episode sharding plus batched inference, SQLite step/episode recording, deterministic merge, structured failures, and versioned configs/results. Adopt it as a VLA evaluator-provider layer under PEARL rather than as the whole self-improving loop: PEARL must still own run_state/events, dense diagnosis, Trace-to-Memory, next-data requirements, self-evolving test generation, regression/promotion gates, rollback, and real-robot verification. Preserve the upstream status distinction between reproduced, integrated-awaiting-reproduction, partial, and planned support; RoboTwin integration is not yet a reproduced model score, live continuous-inference paths remain experimental, and simulation scores do not establish cross-backend equivalence or sim-to-real gains.

references.title: SmolVLA: A Vision-Language-Action Model for Affordable and Efficient Robotics
references.notes: Reference for the local fallback-policy requirement in an agentic robot brain. Official LeRobot/Hugging Face model card describes a compact VLA with multi-view image, robot state, and optional language inputs, continuous action outputs, flow-matching objective, and intended fine-tuning/deployment through LeRobot on affordable hardware.

references.title: RISE: Self-Improving Robot Policy with Compositional World Model
references.notes: Direct self-improving robot-policy reference. Uses a compositional world model to predict controllable multi-view futures and a progress value model to estimate advantages, then continuously generates imagined rollouts and updates the policy in imaginary space before real-world deployment.

references.title: ENPIRE: Agentic Robot Policy Self-Improvement in the Real World
references.notes: Highest-priority collision reference from the Alchedata WhatsApp thread. ENPIRE frames the missing abstraction for robotics research as a repeatable real-world feedback loop: reset the scene, execute a policy, verify the outcome, and refine the next iteration. Its modules map closely to our loop: Environment for automatic reset/verification, Policy Improvement for refinement, Rollout for single/fleet evaluation, and Evolution for coding agents to analyze logs/literature and improve training or algorithm code. Treat it as direct prior art for real-robot agentic self-improvement; differentiate with AgenticSim's simulation-first task/environment generation, typed Data Requirement specs, governance gates, and WAM/TTT memory adapters.

references.title: Playful Agentic Robot Learning (RATs)
references.notes: Closest reference for the skill-memory side: RATs uses self-directed play before downstream tasks arrive, proposes learnable manipulation tasks, executes Code-as-Policy programs, verifies progress, diagnoses failures, retries, and distills successful behavior into a persistent callable code-skill library. For AgenticSim, borrow the competence-frontier / persistent skill-library idea, but keep our stronger simulator task-pack, failure-trace, and policy-training integration.

references.title: PhyAgentOS: Physical Agent Operation System
references.notes: Runtime architecture analogue from the Alchedata WhatsApp thread. PhyAgentOS positions itself as a protocol-based self-evolving and cross-embodiment framework with Cognitive-Physical decoupling, State-as-a-File, Planner/Critic verification, hardware watchdog execution, dynamic HAL drivers, safety correction, and a LESSONS.md experience library. Relevant to our robot-brain/control-plane story; less directly focused on simulation-first task generation and policy-improvement datasets.

references.title: Intern report: MolmoAct 2 / self-improving direction
references.notes: Internal discussion/report link provided by the user. Use as the local slide reference for the MolmoAct-based self-improving direction; public page content was not fetched in this run.

references.title: ASPIRE: Agentic /Skills Discovery for Robotics
references.notes: NVIDIA GEAR reference for code-as-policy robotic skill discovery. ASPIRE records multimodal rollout traces, diagnoses primitive-level failures, rewrites control programs, re-executes them for validation, and saves reusable fixes into a growing skill library. Use it as the direct prior for PEARL skill/memory/debugging, and differentiate through Open X Sim / Any Sim Transfer, scene-task decoupling, simulator task aggregation, and policy-data feedback rather than only task-to-skill conversion.

references.title: PEARL: Self-Improving Agents for Physical AI proposal deck
references.notes: Canonical internal deck for the current PEARL framing. Key architecture points: a closed-loop self-improving physical-AI flywheel, run_state.json/events.jsonl, skillized operations, MCP scene/code/execute/verify/diagnose tools, layered memory, and an Orchestrator that selects the next action. The next weekly goal is not a static Text2Env demo; it is a 2-3 benchmark self-evolving loop with dense failure analysis and evaluation-set extension.

references.title: ASPIRE Robot Execution Engine screenshot
references.notes: User-provided chat screenshot showing ASPIRE Robot Execution Engine / physical-engine framing across LIBERO, RoboSuite, BEHAVIOR, and real-robot execution. Added as image context to keep the benchmark/backend comparison visible.

references.title: Embodied harness loop diagram
references.notes: User-provided harness concept figure. Use it as image context for the Self-Improving / PEARL article positioning: current harness h_t runs embodied tasks; weakness mining clusters execution failures; the harness proposes edits over prompts/tools/memory/policies/validators; proposal validation accepts only regression-tested candidates; accepted edits update h_{t+1} while the embodied model/policy remains the measured object.

risks_decisions: [KNOWN][HIGH] 2026-07-14 diagnosis evidence: 20 place_container_plate rollouts on the RTX 4090 workstation reached 65% success with 7 failures; the next-step deck calls for deterministic failure metrics and video temporal analysis instead of isolated-keyframe diagnosis.

[INFERRED][HIGH] 2026-07-15 scope decision: keep two collaborating workstreams rather than calling both teams generic agent groups. Policy Improvement & Diagnosis owns data/training/evaluation/diagnosis; Environment Generation & Simulation Interoperability owns Text2Env, Anchor2Env, autonomous asset discovery/import, and Open-X-Sim. The /collect and /diagnose contracts remain joint interface surfaces.

Gen-env route contract update 2026-07-09: Alchedata workspace `/home/jingxiang/workspace/alchedata-self-improving-agents` on `jingxiang-b850m-c` now has a unified `/gen-env` route schema at `schemas/gen_env.schema.json`, validated by three contract examples under `artifacts/gen_env_contract/`: `selection2env_route_sample.json`, `forge_fallback_route_blocker.json`, and `material_sidecar_route_blocker.json`. Remote verification passes with `docs=8 static_runs=4 asset_smoke_runs=3 basetask_smoke_runs=3 collect_dry_runs=3 official_rollouts=3 generated_rollouts=2 generated_collections=2 articraft_assets=9996 articraft_archive_passes=3 articraft_collision_blockers=2 act_hdf5=6 act_train=pass_act_train_smoke policy_probe=blocked_policy_train_eval_not_wired gen_env_contracts=3 fallback_blockers=2 artifacts=4`. This covers the schema requirement for selection2env, video2sim-forge fallback, and NeuMaTeX-style material-sidecar routes; it still does not claim actual forge/material execution or generated-task policy evaluation. Commits: local `ea5d211`, remote `86e97f1`.

Gen-env fallback blocker update 2026-07-09: Alchedata workspace `/home/jingxiang/workspace/alchedata-self-improving-agents` on `jingxiang-b850m-c` now records typed `/gen-env` fallback blockers for both required fallback branches. `artifacts/generation_fallback/blocked_drawer_mug_video2sim_forge.json` records `FORGE_CAPTURE_SOURCE_MISSING` for the drawer/mug video2sim-forge route; `artifacts/generation_fallback/blocked_neumatex_material_sidecar.json` records `MATERIAL_MULTIVIEW_INPUT_MISSING` for the NeuMaTeX-style material sidecar route; `fallback_blocker_summary.json` records `blocked_fallback_inputs_missing`. Remote verification passes with `docs=8 static_runs=4 asset_smoke_runs=3 basetask_smoke_runs=3 collect_dry_runs=3 official_rollouts=3 generated_rollouts=2 generated_collections=2 articraft_assets=9996 articraft_archive_passes=3 articraft_collision_blockers=2 act_hdf5=6 act_train=pass_act_train_smoke policy_probe=blocked_policy_train_eval_not_wired fallback_blockers=2 artifacts=4`. This is blocker evidence, not forge/material execution. Commits: local `6653895`, remote `7e32d3d`.

ACT train-smoke update 2026-07-09: Alchedata workspace `/home/jingxiang/workspace/alchedata-self-improving-agents` on `jingxiang-b850m-c` now has six generated planner-trace episodes converted into ACT-compatible HDF5 under `runs/act_hdf5_generated_smoke/`, `load_data_report.json` records `pass_act_hdf5_loader_smoke`, and `runs/act_train_smoke_generated/train_smoke_report.json` records `pass_act_train_smoke` with `best_val_loss=10.308693` and `policy_best.ckpt`. Remote verification passes with `act_hdf5=6 act_train=pass_act_train_smoke policy_probe=blocked_policy_train_eval_not_wired`. Remaining blocker is `/evaluate` and default entrypoint wiring: `process_data.sh` still targets `../../data/task_apple_plate/demo_clean/data`, default train task `sim-task_apple_plate-demo_clean-3` is absent from ACT config, and eval lacks generated `envs.task_apple_plate`. Commits: local `7545109`, remote `ae02653`.

Embodied-harness paper-positioning decision 2026-07-07: frame PEARL as the first real embodied harness target, not merely a self-improving simulator, Text2Env tool, code-repair loop, or VLA training recipe. The defendable claim requires a harness-level abstraction around a fixed embodied model/policy: weakness mining from robot/simulator execution traces, clustered failure patterns, harness proposal edits over prompts/tools/memory/policies/validators, regression tests, and promotion gates that update the harness only when embodied task behavior improves without regression.

NeuMaTeX material-sidecar decision 2026-07-06: texture/material realism should become an explicit /gen-env branch instead of being hidden inside geometry generation. Reference: https://nvlabs.github.io/neumatex/. Use NeuMaTeX-style multi-view neural-material extraction for relightable texture/material sidecars when assets are forged from RGB-D/video, but keep it gated separately from simulator validity: scale, mesh, collision proxy, URDF/joints, physical material parameters, renderer import, and verifier smoke must still pass before /collect uses the scene.

Backend decision 2026-07-03: keep RoboTwin / SAPIEN as the v0 implementation backend rather than forcing Isaac Sim/USD. Isaac/Omniverse remains an adapter target through /transfer, but the near-term system should trust the current RoboTwin stack and avoid turning simulator compatibility into the main blocker.

Scene/task decoupling decision 2026-07-03: user chat argues current ASPIRE/RoboTwin-style systems often generate task and scene together. PEARL should explicitly separate scene generation/selection from task specification so a scene can be reused for multiple operations and task prompts do not force duplicated environment construction.

ASPIRE differentiation decision 2026-07-03: ASPIRE already covers rollout-trace debugging, code-as-policy program repair, evolutionary search, and persistent robot skill libraries. Do not position PEARL as just another task-to-skill system. Position PEARL as Open X Sim / Any Sim Transfer plus MCP-governed simulator aggregation: one reusable scene can support multiple tasks, existing environments can be imported instead of rebuilt, and failure traces become data requirements for /gen-env, /collect, /train, and /evaluate.

video2sim-forge decision 2026-07-06: keep selection2env as the first /gen-env route, but add an explicit forge fallback when the current asset catalog cannot satisfy a scene/task. Reference repo: https://github.com/Marvelousp4/video2sim-forge. The fallback should consume RGB-D capture or reference video and emit simulator-ready asset sidecars: object prompts, masks, meshes, poses, URDFs, approximate physics metadata, and provenance. It is not a policy-training system; generated assets must still pass RoboTwin/SAPIEN import, collision, scale, support, and verifier smoke gates before /collect uses them.
Scope decision: simulation-first MVP; external validation comes after the executable sim loop has gates and replayable reports.
Benchmark decision: start from the completed 129-environment AgenticSim foundation.

Candidate-base decision: mention Genesis World 1.0 and RoboTwin 2.0 as future simulator / benchmark bases, but keep them explicitly separate from the implemented LeHome / RoboLab foundation until adapters and verifier contracts are built.

Dual-Sim access note 2026-06-11: https://github.com/chengaopro/Dual-Sim was user-provided but publicly returned 404 during this update. Treat it as a high-value private/soon-to-open adapter reference; first acceptance step is access confirmation before claiming any code-level merge.

Execution risk: generated scenes and ArtiCraft3D-style generated assets must be executable, reproducible, physically plausible, and gated by verifier / novelty / difficulty checks; TTT must improve held-out accepted scenes rather than overfit one generated scene.

Video2Sim2Real adoption decision 2026-06-09: borrow its object-centric anchor and sim2real adaptation decomposition, but do not make the whole project depend on dexterous-hand demonstrations. AgenticSim should first implement the interface contract on one tabletop task: video/digital-twin manifest, object-keyframe anchors, robot-anchor optimization, recalibration dataset, residual adaptation hook, and real/sim verifier outputs.

SceneSmith-lite decision 2026-06-13: do not attempt full-room SceneSmith first. The current target is a RoboTwin2 tabletop Text2Env demo; done means executable task program, verifier-readable env description, simulator smoke, data-collection dry run, and Pi / OpenVLA-OFT train/eval hook, not a pretty scene-only asset.

Text2Env review decision 2026-07-01: before overbuilding SceneAgent, write a literature review that separates text-to-task, text-to-scene, text-to-asset, selection2env, generation2env, simulation-in-the-loop repair, and policy-data generation. The review should decide which prior systems are implementation references versus only positioning references.

SceneAgent decision 2026-07-01: after the RoboTwin2 Placement/Text2Env demo, the next TODO is selection2env, not generation2env. Zheng Ye should make the agent choose feasible existing RoboTwin/AgenticSim/Articraft-10K assets and placements from task intent, then emit Text2Env JSON / task-program inputs; full asset generation and SAPIEN/Gaussian scene import stay as research blockers.

Orchestrator/blackboard decision 2026-06-13: describe the project as a five-agent closed loop rather than a single Text2Env tool. Text2Env is the first Env Setup implementation, while Diagnosis/Analysis remains the novel core that writes targeted Data Requirement specs back into setup.

WAM / TTT decision 2026-06-13: do not frame the near-term work as full RL post-training. With only a four-L40S budget on the current collaborator side, first test memory-bank critic plus lightweight adapter/head updates; keep RL as a later baseline after single-task fast-improvement evidence exists.

RoboLab data risk 2026-06-13: the 3600 rollout corpus is only useful if metadata records task id, difficulty, seed, policy checkpoint, success/failure, episode length, action distribution, observation/state retrieval keys, and log/artifact paths. Without those fields, memory-bank retrieval and critic estimates will be hard to audit.

Next decision 2026-07-01: lock the selection2env artifact format: accepted asset manifest, rejected asset candidates, placement constraints, VLM scene-feasibility critique, generated Text2Env JSON, generated/updated RoboTwin task-program inputs, smoke/data-collection logs, and generation2env blocker memo.

MolmoAct 2 base decision: promising as an open reasoning/action backbone because weights, data, code, training recipe, and LeRobot integration are available, but it is not itself a self-improving loop. We need an adapter for our robot/action space and a closed-loop AgenticSim finetune/eval recipe before treating it as the main base.

MolmoAct 2 limitation to verify: current public framing notes batch-style action planning and strongest support for SO-100, bimanual YAM, and Franka-like setups. Continuous replanning, hand-equipped robots, and our exact embodiments require additional data or adapters.

Robot-brain architecture decision 2026-06-09: treat the robot controller as a modular agentic brain. Route short/simple jobs to specialized small policies or classical optimization; route long-horizon jobs through VLM task decomposition; let each subtask choose optimizer, VLA, skill API, simulator check, or human escalation.

System 0/1/2 decision 2026-06-09: organize the robot-brain story around latency and context. System 0 owns fast embodied reflexes from proprioception/tactile/current/force signals; System 1 owns quick short-history action decisions; System 2 owns memory-heavy reasoning and planning. The hidden latent mediator is the key interface to design, because it decides what low-level body state reaches planning and what high-level intent can safely modulate reflex/action layers.

Edge fallback decision 2026-06-09: the system should not go actionless when network/cloud VLM access fails. Keep a local small VLA/policy fallback such as SmolVLA-style LeRobot policy for safe stop, retry, retreat, and bounded primitive execution.

InternDataEngine adoption risk: useful as a synthetic-data production reference, but license / Isaac Sim stack / cluster scheduling requirements mean it should start as a design reference until a small compatible generation job is tested inside AgenticSim.

RISE collision/differentiation risk: it already covers self-improving robot policy via imagination. Our angle should be MolmoAct-style reasoning VLA plus agentic scene/task generation, trace memory, governance gates, and executable task-pack feedback rather than only imagined RL.

ENPIRE collision/differentiation risk 2026-06-21: ENPIRE directly overlaps with the agentic real-robot self-improvement loop and has stronger real-world evidence, including automatic reset/evaluation, rollout scaling, and coding-agent evolution. Do not pitch AgenticSim as simply the same reset-rollout-evolve loop. Pitch it as the simulation-first generator/evaluator/memory substrate that can produce typed tasks, failure traces, Data Requirement specs, WAM/TTT adaptation evidence, and governance-checked task packs before a real-robot ENPIRE-style harness is available.

RATs adoption decision 2026-06-21: use RATs as the reference for autonomous play and persistent callable code skills, but keep our acceptance stricter: skills must be linked to simulator state, failure taxonomy, verifier evidence, task-pack provenance, and downstream policy/eval deltas, not only reusable Code-as-Policy snippets.

PhyAgentOS boundary 2026-06-21: useful for transparent cognitive-physical decoupling and file/protocol-mediated hardware execution. It should inform our runtime/control-plane design, but it is not enough for the current proposal unless paired with AgenticSim environment generation, policy training/evaluation hooks, and failure-to-data-requirement loops.
Execution routing 2026-06-05: Skill World Model work is the SkillCall memory gate; RLBench->IsaacSim work is the AgenticSim Isaac verifier pilot.

Bridge boundary 2026-06-05: contact-rich VTLA/UMI traces can drive curriculum and failure-memory design, but Self-improving should not depend on tactile hardware until the sim-first oracle or available sensor path is verified.

[USER-SUPPLIED PHASE 2 PLAN 2026-07-22] Rewrote the seven existing environment-generation TODOs instead of creating duplicates. Primary owners: Hu = Onboarding/Harness/Transfer; Zheng Ye = Text2Env/Anchor2Env; Shao Gujie = Asset reuse/Generation fallback. Dependency chain: Onboarding -> public interfaces + Harness -> Text2Env and Asset reuse -> Anchor2Env and Generation fallback -> Transfer -> full regression. Still pending team confirmation: shared simulator backend and second adapter, interface freeze date, and first end-to-end integration date.

timeline.target_note: [FRAME] Current target: make all four routes callable through the same versioned harness, preserve reuse-versus-generation decisions and typed evidence, and block promotion when provenance, physics, replay or migration-loss gates fail.

subprojects.name: Phase 2. Agentic Harness / Skills + MCP
subprojects.summary: [FRAME] Integrate the four accepted V1 routes behind a reusable, versioned Skill/MCP registry with selection-first asset routing, rigid/articulated generation fallback, typed artifacts, bounded repair and regression-gated promotion.

subprojects.name: 1. Text2Env
subprojects.summary: [COMPUTED] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] The bounded V1 Stage 0-5 gate passes; Phase 2 adapter packaging is tracked separately and does not reopen the historical acceptance evidence.

subprojects.name: 2. Image / Video Anchor2Env
subprojects.summary: [COMPUTED] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] The bounded V1 three-scene image/video gate passes; Phase 2 Skill/MCP packaging and reuse-first fallback routing remain separate active work.

subprojects.name: 3. Asset Reuse + 3D Generation Fallback
subprojects.summary: [COMPUTED] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] The bounded V1 three-source and ten-asset discovery/import gate passes. [FRAME] Phase 2 splits verified retrieval and parameter filling from rigid/articulated generative fallback.

subprojects.name: 4. Open-X-Sim Cross-Simulator Reuse
subprojects.summary: [COMPUTED] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] The bounded V1 rigid, articulated and camera-sensitive L0-L3 gate passes. [KNOWN] [CONFIDENCE: HIGH] Reusable Skill/MCP packaging and L4 controller or policy equivalence remain outside that completion.
